### 1. Understand the Syllabus and Exam Pattern

The first step in preparing for GATE 2026 is to know the syllabus and the exam structure properly. Each branch has its own subjects, but Engineering Mathematics and General Aptitude are common for all. The exam has multiple-choice questions, numerical answer-type questions, and questions that test your analytical ability.

 Read the complete GATE syllabus of your branch and mark the topics.
 Divide the subjects into strong, medium, and weak areas.
 Note that General Aptitude and Mathematics carry good weightage.
 Use a copy of the syllabus while studying so you do not miss anything.

title: SSC CGL Average Questions Solved Problems with Detailed ...
content:
Previous Year Papers [...] #### SSC CGL Average Questions Question 7:

The average of 41 numbers is 62. The average of the first 21 numbers is 56 and that of the last 21 numbers is 70. If the 21st number is removed, then what will be the average of the remaining numbers?

1. 64.25
2. 66.75
3. 68.85
4. 60.95

#### Answer (Detailed Solution Below)

Option 4 : 60.95

#### SSC CGL Average Questions Question 7 Detailed Solution

Given:

The average of the 41 number = 62

Formula used:

Average = (The sum of all the items)/(The total number of items)

Calculation:

Let us assume the average of the remaining numbers be X

⇒ The total sum of 41 numbers = 41 × 62 = 2542

⇒ The total sum of the first 21 numbers = 21 × 56 = 1176

⇒ The total sum of last 21 numbers = 21 × 70 = 1470 [...] #### SSC CGL Average Questions Question 5:

The average of 23 numbers is 51. The average of first 12 numbers is 49 and the average of last 12 numbers is 54. If the twelfth number is removed, then the average of the remaining numbers (correct to two decimal places) is:

1. 50.45
2. 53.25
3. 51.75
4. 52.65

#### Answer (Detailed Solution Below)

Option 1 : 50.45

#### SSC CGL Average Questions Question 5 Detailed Solution

Given:

The average of 23 numbers = 51

The average of first 12 numbers = 49

The average of last 12 numbers = 54

Formula used:

Average = sum of observations/Number of observations

Calculation:

The sum of 23 numbers = (23 × 51) = 1173

The sum of first 12 numbers = (12 × 49) = 588

The sum of last 12 numbers = (12 × 54) = 648

Now,

title: SSC Profit and Loss Questions Solved Problems with Detailed ...
content:
Previous Year Papers [...] Test Series [...] #### SSC Profit and Loss Questions Question 7:

A shopkeeper increases the price of an object by 40% and then sells it at a 25% discount on the marked price. If the selling price of such an object be Rs. 2100, its cost price for the shopkeeper was?

1. 3000
2. 1500
3. 1750
4. 2000

#### Answer (Detailed Solution Below)

Option 4 : 2000

#### SSC Profit and Loss Questions Question 7 Detailed Solution

25% = 1/4 and 40% = 2/5

SP of the article = Rs. 2100

MP of the book = 2100 × (4/3)

∴ CP of the book = 2100 × (4/3) × (5/7) = Rs. 2000

content:
Previous Year Papers [...] #### SSC CGL Profit and Loss Questions Question 6:

A, B and C invested ₹40,000, ₹48,000 and ₹80,000, respectively, for a business at the start of a year. After six months, for the remaining time of the year, A added ₹4,000, B added ₹4,000 while C withdrew ₹4,000 every month. If the total profit is ₹6,72,000, then what is C's share (in ₹)?

1. 1,96,750
2. 1,80,480
3. 2,11,200
4. 2,80,320

#### Answer (Detailed Solution Below)

Option 4 : 2,80,320

#### SSC CGL Profit and Loss Questions Question 6 Detailed Solution

Given:

A, B, and C invested ₹40000, ₹48000, and ₹80000, respectively

The total profit = ₹ 672000

Calculation:

After six months, for the remaining time of the year, A added ₹4000, B added ₹4000 and C withdrew ₹4000 every month. [...] #### SSC CGL Profit and Loss Questions Question 7:

A sold a mobile phone to B at a gain of 25% and B sold it to C at a loss of 10%. If C paid Rs. 5,625 for it, how much did A pay (in Rs.) for the phone?

1. 4,500
2. 4,800
3. 5,100
4. 5,000

#### Answer (Detailed Solution Below)

Option 4 : 5,000

#### SSC CGL Profit and Loss Questions Question 7 Detailed Solution

Given:

A sold a mobile phone to B at a gain of 25%

B sold the mobile to C at a loss of 10%

Total money paid by C = Rs. 5625

Calculation:

Let, the A paid for the mobile phone = x

So, x × (125/100) × (90/100) = 5625

⇒ x = 5625 × (100/125) × (100/90)

⇒ x = 5000

∴ A paid Rs. 5000 (in Rs.) for the phone

## SSC GD Profit & Loss Questions and Answers

Q.1 An article was sold at ₹600, making a profit of 20%. The cost price of the article was

A. ₹720  
 B. ₹640  
 C. ₹500  
 D. ₹480

Answer: C

Solution:  
 SP = ₹600  
 Profit = 20%

SP = CP × (1 + 20/100)  
 600 = CP × 1.20  
 CP = 600 ÷ 1.20 = ₹500

Q.2 A shopkeeper allows a discount of 20% on the marked price. What price must be marked on an article costing ₹3360, so as to make a gain of 25%?

A. ₹5300  
 B. ₹5250  
 C. ₹5050  
 D. ₹4950

Answer: B

Solution:  
 CP = ₹3360  
 Gain = 25%

SP = 3360 × 125/100 = ₹4200  
 0.8 × MP = 4200  
 MP = 4200 ÷ 0.8 = ₹5250

Q.3 A laptop is marked at ₹50,000. A shopkeeper offers a festival discount of 10%, followed by an additional discount of 8% on the reduced price. What is the final selling price? [...] Let new MP = x  
 0.95x = 1235  
 x = 1235 ÷ 0.95 = ₹1300

Q.28 92 mirrors were purchased for ₹92. During transit, 35 broke. Remaining mirrors were sold at ₹4.40 each. Find the profit.

A. ₹158.80  
 B. ₹157.90  
 C. ₹161.70  
 D. ₹153.10

Answer: A

Solution:  
 Mirrors sold = 92 − 35 = 57

Selling price = 57 × 4.40 = 250.80  
 Total cost price = 92

Profit = 250.80 − 92 = ₹158.80

Q.29 A person buys a ceiling fan for ₹2200 and sells it at a loss of 35%. Find the selling price.

A. ₹1410  
 B. ₹1430  
 C. ₹1420  
 D. ₹1440

Answer: B

Solution:  
 Selling Price = 2200 × (1 − 35/100)  
 = 2200 × 0.65  
 = ₹1430

Q.30 On a marked price, the difference between the selling prices with a single discount of 40% and successive discounts of 15% and 20% is ₹400. Find the marked price. [...] A. 110%  
 B. 95%  
 C. 100%  
 D. 105%

Answer: C

Solution:  
 Let SP of 1 chair = ₹1  
 Total SP = 150  
 Gain = 75

CP = 150 − 75 = 75  
 Profit % = (75 ÷ 75) × 100 = 100%

Q.17 While selling 15 balls for ₹1220, there is a loss equal to the cost price of 5 balls. Find the cost price of one ball.

A. 145  
 B. 122  
 C. 112  
 D. 120

Answer: B

Solution:  
 Let CP of one ball = x

SP of 15 balls = CP of 15 balls − CP of 5 balls  
 1220 = 15x − 5x  
 1220 = 10x  
 x = 122

Q.18 A merchant marks his goods 50% above cost price and allows a discount of 12%. Find the profit percentage.

A. 37%  
 B. 32%  
 C. 43%  
 D. 35%

Answer: B

Solution:  
 Let CP = 100  
 MP = 150  
 SP = 150 × 0.88 = 132

Profit = 132 − 100 = 32  
 Profit % = 32%

title: SSC CGL Time And Work Previous Year Questions PDF - Cracku
content:
18) Answer (C)

Total work in terms of Boy days i.e only boys working is 5\6=30 BD  
 Total work in terms of Men days i.e only men working is 6\4=24 MD  
 Therefore 30 BD=24 MD  
 1 BD=4/5 MD  
 So total work in terms of given people is 2x BD+2x MD=30 BD  
 2x BD + 2x\(5/4) BD=30 BD  
 (9/2)x=30  
 x=20/3 days

19) Answer (D)

Total work to de done in terms of MenDays and WomenDays is  
 (3\5)MD+(5\5)WD=15 MD+25 WD……..1  
 (2\7)MD+(4\7)WD=14MD+28 WD  
 15 MD+25 WD=14 MD+28 WD  
 1 MD=3 WD

substituting 1 MD=3 WD in the 1st equation

Total work is 70 WD i.e 15\3 WD+25 WD= 70 WD

Given 2 Men and 2 Women and so 2\3\x WD+2\x WD=70 WD

8x=70 WD  
 x=70/8 days

20) Answer (B)

Speed of the train relative to the man= $\Large\frac{450}{36}$ m/sec  
 = $\Large\frac{25}{2}$ m/sec. [...] 10) Answer (A)

Total units of work that has to be done is lcm of 9 and 27 i.e 27 units.  
 Each day number of units of work done by W is 27/9 =3 units.  
 Each day number of units of work done by X is 27/27=1 unit  
 So on day 1 X starts and so on day1 1 unit of work is done, day 2 W will work and so 3 units i.e (1+3) units of work is done for 2 days and so total number of units of work to be done is 27 and so in 12 days 24 units of work is done and on 13th day 1 unit is done by X and so 2 units is left it can be done in (2/3)by W and so toal 13(2/3) days is required.

11) Answer (B) [...] 16) Answer (A)

They both can complete the whole work in x days i.e  
 1/17 + 1/51 =1/x  
 x=51/4 days  
 For 50% of the work to get completed it takes (51/4)\(1/2)=51/8 days.

17) Answer (A)

Given 1/A + 1/B =1/19  
 1/B + 1/C =1/95  
 Subtracting these equations we get  
 1/A – 1/C = 4/95  
 Given that N alone takes 52 days to complete the work and so we have  
 1/A – 1/76 =1/95  
 1/A=1/95 + 1/76  
 1/A=9/380  
 A=380/9 days  
 The time taken for A and C to complete the work by working together is  
 1/(380/9) + 1/76 =7/190  
 190/7 days.

18) Answer (C)
